Workflow Features Screens Download About
One file · works offline · free forever

From screenplay to bid
to crew schedule.

BidCraft is a free, single-file tool for VFX supervisors and producers. Tag a script into a shot breakdown, build a department-by-department bid, model reusable AI workflows, compare bidding rounds, and auto-build the crew ramp — all in your browser, fully offline. Your scripts and numbers never leave your machine.

Download BidCraft See how it works
BidCraft — Bidding

Live example: a 186-shot alien-invasion feature, 23 assets, full department bid.

One tool, six connected pages

Everything flows from the script: tag once, and your shots carry through the dashboard, bidding, review, comparison and scheduling.

Overview

A live dashboard: total bid, cost-per-day, the artist / support / overhead / AI budget split, and sequence & asset charts.

Breakdown

Paste or import a script, format it, select effects and tag them with categories, complexity, assets and notes.

Bidding

Every shot & asset becomes a spreadsheet row, every department a column. Days, rates, templates, live totals.

Review

Flag shots for discussion and capture Producer & VFX-Sup Q&A — built for spotting sessions.

Compare

Snapshot a round, then see exactly what moved between cuts — by scene, shot and asset.

Schedule

Set key dates, auto-fill a crew ramp from the bid, and plan support crew with a running budget.

Built for real bidding

Years of script-breakdown and bidding practice, distilled into one lightweight file.

Tag straight from the script

Highlight the words describing an effect and tag with custom VFX categories, complexity levels, reusable assets, and assumption/methodology notes. Highlights link back to their shot.

Spreadsheet-grade bidding

A Google-Sheets-style grid with frozen columns, keyboard navigation, № multipliers, producer offsets, day rates and live cost totals — in days or weeks. Hide or reorder department columns, and shift-click to select a range of rows.

Bid templates & bulk actions

Save a bid as a template and apply it across selected rows, or use bulk actions to set complexity, tag, park, flag, duplicate or delete many shots at once.

Crew scheduling & auto-fill

Set start, turnovers, delivery and your own custom milestones, then auto-fill a per-department weekly ramp from the bid — flat, only-empty, or a phased ramp with curve shapes (ramp, bell, plateau, ramp-up), head caps and studio-closure awareness. Add a support-crew plan with a live budget.

Snapshots & compare

Freeze each bidding round and compare any two — color-coded day and cost deltas by scene, shot and asset, so you always see where the budget moved.

Import & export

Import a script PDF or a shot list from Excel/CSV. Export a full Excel workbook (breakdown, summary, bid, schedule, plus a dedicated AI Setups sheet), CSV, ShotGrid-ready files, or a standalone AI Setups file.

Private by design

No install, no login, no server, no telemetry. Everything stays in your browser — safe for confidential, unreleased scripts.

Tailor it to the job

Toggle whole modules on or off (budgeting, scheduling), hide all prices for screen-sharing, and switch between dark and light themes.

Yours to keep

Save and load complete project files, or export a reusable studio settings file (tags, departments, rates, presets) to standardise every new show.

AI Setups

Model reusable AI workflows as their own bidding tab: a one-time build cost (software, GPU, build days) plus a per-use offset that can be a net saving. Apply across shots or assets, with per-entry overrides, its own dashboard budget slice, and its own line in the crew ramp.

Read & mark your script

Drop colour-coded bookmarks anywhere in the script — each timestamped and labelled from nearby text — and click to jump straight back. Format raw text into clean screenplay layout in one click. Bookmarks save with the project.

See it in action

Real screens from the bundled example project.

BidCraft — Tag the script
Breakdown

Tag the script

Paste or import the screenplay, format it into clean layout, then select the text describing each effect and tag it. Scene auto-fills, shot numbers auto-increment, and each tagged shot becomes a card — with assumptions, methodology, complexity and assets attached.

BidCraft — Plan the crew
Schedule

Plan the crew

Set the start, turnovers, delivery and your own custom milestones — each colour-coded and mirrored as a line on the ramp. Auto-fill a per-department weekly head-count ramp from the bid (flat, only-empty, or a phased curve), tracked against the bid budget, then add support crew with a running cost.

BidCraft — Track every round
Compare

Track every round

Snapshot the bid at the end of each round. When the next cut arrives, compare any two snapshots to see the day and cost deltas — rolled up by scene, then broken down by shot and asset, colour-coded for increases and decreases.

BidCraft — Overview
Overview

See the whole show at a glance

A live dashboard updates as you bid: total shots, bid days, cost-per-day and total cost, with AI broken out as its own figure. The budget split shows artist & assets, support crew, overhead and AI as proportional segments — and ranked charts reveal where days are concentrated by department, sequence and asset.

BidCraft — AI Setups
AI Setups

Model reusable AI workflows

Build an AI tool once — software, GPU and build days — then apply it across shots and assets with a per-use offset that can be a net saving. Each setup shows its build cost (counted once), per-shot effect, usage count and net total, carries its own line into the crew ramp, and exports to a dedicated sheet.

Get BidCraft

Completely free — no registration, no subscriptions, no ads. Download the single HTML file and open it in any modern browser, or grab the installable app package and example project to explore.

Download the app single .html file · right-click → save as
View on GitHub

Installable app package

A PWA zip to run BidCraft in its own window, fully offline.

Example project

A complete 186-shot demo show — load it to explore every page.

User manual

A full PDF / editable manual covering every feature.

Runs in Chrome, Edge, Safari and Firefox. No data ever leaves your machine. My gift to the industry.

Mathieu Vierendeel

Visual Effects Producer

VFX professional with over 17 years in the industry. I built BidCraft to help fellow supervisors and producers turn scripts into accurate bids and schedules — fast, private, and without expensive software.

It started as a tagging tool and grew, with feedback from the floor, into a full breakdown-to-schedule workflow. It's free, and it always will be.
